Policies on Faculty Workload

I. Rationale

Jose Rizal Memorial State University commits to provide quality instruction to all its students delivered by qualified and competent faculty members. With the influx of students in the University due to the free higher education, the student population has increased significantly. Hence, this impacts the faculty workload and eventually requires additional teaching force for the various program offerings across campuses. Having operated for more than ten (10) years as a university, it has encountered a number of challenges which have been recurring and unresolved since the existing policies on faculty workload are limited in nature. Hence, the university needs to update and align its institutional policies especially on the regular teaching load of permanent faculty members including the payment of honorarium and overtime pay for the teaching overload pursuant to the 2024 Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) issued by the Commission on Audit.

II. Policy Statements

Section 1 In accordance with the Article 83 of the Labor Code of the Philippines, as amended, the normal hours of work of school personnel shall not exceed eight (8) hours a day.

Any work done in addition to the eight (8) hours daily work shall constitute overtime work (CMO 40, s.2008; CMO 30, s.2009).

Section 2 Each head of the department in the SUC may allow flexible working hours for his/her faculty members so they can perform their four-fold functions of instruction, consultation, research and extension services, provided that the prescribed forty hours of work per week shall strictly be implemented (CSC MC No. 19, s.2005).

Section 3 Agencies in government may adopt compressed workweek that refers to a work arrangement whereby the forty (40) hours workweek for five (5) days of the government officials or employees is compressed to four (4) days or less, as may be applicable (CSC MC No. 06, s.2022).

Section 4 The regular teaching load of full-time academic teaching personnel shall be determined by the higher education

qa_pairs.answer: Eight (8) hours a day, as per Article 83 of the Labor Code of the Philippines.

qa_pairs.answer: Yes, each head of department may allow flexible working hours for their faculty members, provided that the prescribed forty hours of work per week are strictly implemented.

qa_pairs.answer: A work arrangement whereby the forty (40) hours workweek for five (5) days of the government officials or employees is compressed to four (4) days or less, as may be applicable.

qa_pairs.answer: The regular teaching load of full-time academic teaching personnel shall be determined by the higher education institution.

qa_pairs.answer: The payment of honorarium and overtime pay for teaching overload is based on the 2024 Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) issued by the Commission on Audit.

qa_pairs.answer: The strict constraints for faculty workload include the normal hours of work of school personnel, any work done in addition to the eight hours daily work constituting overtime work, and the prescribed forty hours of work per week.

qa_pairs.answer: The policy on faculty workload affects the student population by providing additional teaching force for various program offerings across campuses, thereby impacting the student population.

qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the policy on faculty workload can be updated or aligned as needed.

qa_pairs.question: What is the goal of the policy on faculty workload?
qa_pairs.answer: The goal of the policy on faculty workload is to provide quality instruction to all students delivered by qualified and competent faculty members.

Section 5 Any teaching load in excess of the normal or regular teaching load shall be considered as overload. Overload partakes of the nature of temporary extra assignment and compensation therefore shall be considered as an overload honorarium if within the 8-hour work period and does not form part of the regular or basic pay.

Overload performed beyond the eight-hour daily work is overtime work (CMO 40, s.2008; CMO 30, s.2009).

Section 6 Honoraria may be paid to teaching personnel engaged in actual classroom teaching, whose teaching load is outside of the regular office hours or in excess of the regular load (Sec.63 (a) General Provisions of RA 11975).

Section 7 Government employees whose regular schedule of work falls between 6 a.m. and 6 p.m., including those under the flexible work arrangements whose nature of services requires their rendition within the same period are excluded from the grant of Night Shift Differential Pay. The services they rendered beyond the regular eight (8)-hour work schedules shall be compensated in accordance with the existing laws, rules, and regulations on overtime services and overtime pay (CSC-DBM-GCGOCCs Joint Circular No. 1, s.2023).

7.1 An employee whose prescribed work schedule is from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m., but renders services until 7 p.m., as authorized by the head of agency. The hours worked from 6 p.m. to 7 p.m. shall not be covered by Night Shift Differential Pay, but shall be compensated in accordance with the rules and regulations on overtime services and overtime pay.

7.2 An employee whose agency implements compressed workweek with ten (10) working hours for four days in a week, has the prescribed work schedule from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m., with 12 p.m. to 1 p.m. as the time for lunch. The hours worked from 6 p.m. to 7 p.m. shall not be covered by Night Shift Differential Pay. In case the employee renders services beyond the prescribed ten (10) hours of work, as authorized by the head of agency, he/she shall be compensated in accordance with the rules and regulations on overtime services and overtime pay.

III. Terms Defined

Compensatory Overtime Credits (COCs) – are any units of WL aside from actual teaching load in excess of 24 units/ term. These are pooled/lumped into compensatory time-offs (CTOS) by the HRMO that can be used by any faculty to cover his/her absences or undertime.

Seasonal/Occasional Designations – refer to designations which do not require daily and weekly client engagement, and monthly submissions of reportorial requirements and conduct of office-initiated activities.

Teaching Load (TL) refers to actual teaching loads given to faculty members measured in hours load of subjects to teach (under a. instruction) assigned to a teacher. It excludes work assignments in b) research, c) extension, d) production and e) others/administrative designations.

V. General Policies

Section 1 All faculty members holding permanent positions/items must first be given regular WL for the semester before additional teachers (under Contract of Service) will be hired. Section 2 Faculty members must be informed of their tentative teaching loads (TL) at least one (1) week before the start of classes. Section 3 The percentage distribution of job function vis-à-vis major thrusts of the University shall be specified in the Individual Performance Commitment and Review (IPCR). Section 4 A faculty member with a WL & TL in excess of 30 hours/week within office hours, after office hours on a scheduled workday, on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day shall be entitled to an honorarium/overtime pay for the teaching overload subject to the availability of funds or is granted service credit equivalence. Section 5 All other assignments with monetary remuneration shall not be given any workload equivalent. Section 6 The IGP Manual of the University shall be used as guide when a faculty member is involved in Production function. Section 7 A faculty member must satisfy the mandatory functions before accepting optional loads. Section 8 In the exigency of the service, additional loads may be given to faculty with more than the minimum WL.

VI. Faculty Workload

Section 1 A regular teaching load of at least twenty-four (24) units a week for each faculty member shall be required, but with a maximum service of forty (40) hours a week including consultation, research, extension and quasi-teaching administrative functions.

Table VI.1 Teaching Preparation

| Faculty without designation | ← |
|---|---|
| Teaching Load (in units) | No. of Teaching Preparation(s) |
| 24 units | 1-2 preparations |
| 21 units | 3-4 preparations |
| 15 units and below | 5 and above |
| Faculty with designation | ← |
| Teaching Load (in units) | No. of Teaching Preparation(s) |
| 18-21 units | not more than 3 preparations |

qa_pairs.question: What is the definition of consultation hours?
qa_pairs.answer: Consultation hours refer to the time a faculty member spends with students outside of regular class hours, such as during office hours or by appointment.

qa_pairs.question: How are cash incentives divided among extensionists in case of budget constraints?
qa_pairs.answer: The cash incentives shall be divided according to usual accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

qa_pairs.question: What is the definition of a faculty member without designation?
qa_pairs.answer: A faculty member without designation refers to a teacher who does not have a specific academic rank or title, such as Professor, Associate Professor, or Assistant Professor.

qa_pairs.question: Can a Dean or Department Chair/Head handle a teaching load that exceeds their designated load?
qa_pairs.answer: The Dean and Department Chair/Head may be allowed to handle a maximum teaching load of not more than 12 hours per week, provided that their primary duties and responsibilities are not neglected.

qa_pairs.question: What is the definition of remuneration?
qa_pairs.answer: Remuneration refers to forms of compensation received in exchange for work or services performed, such as salary, wages, bonuses, or benefits.

VIII. Outside Teaching Load

Section 1 Outside teaching load refers to academic instruction rendered in other colleges or universities. The schedule for outside teaching should be outside the regular official and overload teaching time.

Section 2 Faculty members who wish to teach outside should secure the permission and approval from the program chair, Associate Dean, Dean, Campus Director, Vice President for Academic Affairs, and the University President, respectively.

Section 3 An outside teaching load of not more than nine (9) units for lecture and five (5) units for laboratory may be allowed to a faculty member.

Section 4 Faculty on study leave with pay or on scholarship shall not be allowed to render outside teaching loads.

IX. Separability Clause

Section 1 If, for any reason, any part of provision of this policy and guideline is declared invalid or unconstitutional, the remaining parts or provisions not affected thereby shall remain in full force and effect.

X. Effectivity

Section 1 These Policies on Faculty Workload approved by the JRMSU Board of Regents per Resolution No. 145-2024 dated September 24, 2024 shall take effect this Academic Year 2024-2025.
